instruments.title: Workers with Support Needs
instruments.description: Interview guide for workers with support needs (beneficiaries) — the people the WISE is designed to help integrate into employment.

questions.text: What is your role here, and how did you come to be involved with this organisation? When did you join? How did you first hear about it? Who helped you to join?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: niche creation, recruitment pathways

questions.text: How long are you able to work in this organisation? How do you feel about the time limits, if any, on your stay?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: integration model (transitional vs permanent)

questions.text: Did you work in another job before working for this WISE? In the public, private for-profit or nonprofit sector? How does working here compare to your previous work experience(s)?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: trajectory, labour market context

questions.text: What does a typical working day look like for you? Describe your day-to-day activities. Are you happy with your work conditions?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: working conditions, job quality

questions.text: What do you enjoy most about your work here? Do you feel respected and valued here as a worker? Why or why not?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: worker wellbeing, empowerment

questions.text: Is there anything you find particularly difficult/challenging in your work? Is there anything you would change about your work here?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: challenges, areas for improvement

questions.text: Have you learned new skills since joining? If so, what are those skills?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: skills development, training effectiveness

questions.text: Do you know how decisions are taken in the organisation? Are you asked your opinions about how things work in the WISE? Are you invited to help make decisions?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: participation, democratic governance

questions.text: How has being part of this WISE affected your life outside of work? What has changed in your life (e.g. income, independence, self-confidence, relationships, housing, health)?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: social outcomes, broader impact

questions.text: Have broader events or changes (e.g. COVID-19, cost of living, local economic changes, climate change) affected your work or experience here? How has the organisation responded?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: landscape pressures, resilience

questions.text: [If working in the WISE for some time] Did you notice any evolution in the organisation since your work here, in good or in bad?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: organisational change over time

questions.text: Would you like to keep working in the organisation in the future? Do you plan on working for another organisation? If so, what kind of organisation?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: aspirations, progression pathways

questions.text: Do you feel like this organisation cares about the environment? Is it important for you or not?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: environmental awareness, mission alignment

instruments.title: Workers that are Supporters
instruments.description: Interview guide for staff who support and supervise workers with support needs (tutors, job coaches, mentors).

questions.text: What is your role here, and how did you come to be involved with this organisation? How long have you been working in this organisation?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: supporter background, recruitment

questions.text: To what extent is it important for you to work in a work integration social enterprise? In what ways did this inform your decision to accept this role?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: motivation, mission alignment

questions.text: What does your day-to-day work activities look like? Can you briefly describe your tasks? What kinds of support do you provide to Workers with Support Needs?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: support practices, work content

questions.text: What do you find most meaningful — and/or most challenging — about working in this WISE? Can you elaborate on this a bit?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: job satisfaction, challenges

questions.text: How would you describe the level and intensity of support that WSNs receive? How many WSNs do you supervise, and what is the percentage of your time dedicated to supervisory/support tasks?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: support intensity, ratios

questions.text: What are the social backgrounds of your WSNs – what are the main systemic barriers that they face? Did you see those 'WSN profiles' evolve through time?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: target group characteristics, evolution

questions.text: What kind of skills do you feel that the WISE is helping to develop among WSNs (hard vs soft skills, technical vs social skills)? Did that evolve through time?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: skills development, outcomes

questions.text: How do you see the skills and empowerment of the WSNs developing as they work for this WISE? Are you or someone else in charge of assessing the progress of individual WSNs?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: progress tracking, empowerment

questions.text: Does the WISE provide for any training for its workers, both supporters and WSNs? Is the training ongoing? What are some of the drawbacks or challenges?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: training provision, challenges

questions.text: In your opinion, what enables or limits workers' transition to the conventional labour market? How long can 'workers with special needs' work in this WISE?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: transition barriers, integration model

questions.text: How would you describe the mission of the organisation? How did this mission evolve? To what extent are environmental concerns part of this WISE's mission?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: mission, environmental dimension

questions.text: How do you see your role evolving over the next 5-10 years, considering possible changes in the work environment and the needs of WSNs?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: future outlook, adaptability

questions.text: Have you seen some changes in the WISE since you started working here – can you tell me about them please? Have the developments been positive?
Can you tell me about the governance structure of the organisation? Are you involved in key decision-making of this WISE? Please provide some examples.
Are WSNs involved in key decision-making of this WISE? Please provide some examples. Do you feel that they are sufficiently involved?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: democratic governance, WSN participation

questions.text: Do you feel that there is discrimination you face within this and/or in society (racism, sexism, ableism, etc.)? Have you or your colleagues already experienced some forms of discrimination?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: discrimination, social barriers

questions.text: Do you know what kinds of data your organisation collects regarding the social and environmental dimensions of its activities? Do you know if one or several assessment tools are used?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: assessment practices, data collection

instruments.title: Managers / Coordinators
instruments.description: Comprehensive interview guide for WISE managers covering background, mission, economics, governance, policy environment, and ecosystem relations.

questions.text: Could you tell me about your background and what led you to join this work integration social enterprise? What has been your trajectory in the WISE?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: manager profile, pathway

questions.text: How would you describe the mission of this WISE?
Could you describe the work integration mission of the social enterprise? How has the work integration mission evolved over time?
Which are the key target groups? How and why have the target groups evolved over time?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: target group selection, evolution

questions.text: In your view, what are the main skills (basic, technical, social, digital) that WSNs acquire here? What kind of skills do you feel that the WISE is helping to develop?
How do you see the skills and empowerment of the WSNs developing as they work for this WISE? Are you in charge of assessing the progress of individual WSNs?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: empowerment, progress tracking

questions.text: What are the required skills of the supporters (workers supporting WSNs)? Do supporters need to have a formal certification?
Does the WISE provide for any training for its workers, both supporters and WSNs? Is the training ongoing?
Do you feel that there are discrimination issues related to this WISE (racism, sexism, ableism, etc.)?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: discrimination, social barriers

questions.text: Could you tell me the story of this WISE since its creation, who founded it? How has the size evolved over time? Could you identify the most important moments or turning points?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: organisational trajectory, critical junctures

questions.text: What is the legal form of the WISE? Why did the organisation choose this legal form? Do you think it is suitable, or would you like to change it?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: legal form, institutional framework

questions.text: What products or services does your organization provide?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: economic activity, market positioning

questions.text: Why did the organization choose to offer this particular type of goods or services? What motivated this focus?
What is your strategy regarding pricing, why, and how does it align with your mission?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: pricing strategy, mission-economics balance

questions.text: Who are your clients? To what extent are they different from those of your competitors or alternative providers?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: market position, client base

questions.text: What were the major evolutions in your economic model over time?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: economic sustainability, evolution

questions.text: Are there any financial or economic challenges that make it hard for this WISE to fulfil its mission?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: financial challenges, sustainability

questions.text: In light of the stated mission and goals, what would be the ideal economic model (including revenue mix) required to effectively fulfil the mission?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: ideal vs actual economic model

questions.text: What do you see as the main challenges to make such an economic model feasible?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: barriers to sustainability

questions.text: Who do you see as your competitors or alternatives, and how do they influence your choices?
How would you describe the management style in the organization (participative, top-down, etc.)?
What approaches or practices do you use to support and motivate vulnerable workers?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: HR practices, worker support

questions.text: What is the type of governance of your organisation? How are decisions taken? By whom and how?
Could you describe the responsibilities, roles and influence of the board within the WISE?
Do you have volunteers in the WISE? What is their role? How important is it in your view?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: volunteering, workforce composition

questions.text: Do you see this WISE as successful? How do you qualify and measure this success?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: self-assessment, success criteria

questions.text: What challenges do you face in running the WISE?
What do you see as the main advantages or disadvantages of this WISE relative to other WISEs or to other enterprises providing the same type of goods/services?
In what ways would you say that this WISE has a transformative capacity, for its beneficiaries and for the organization itself?
What policies or regulations most affect (positively or negatively) how your organisation operates?
Is your organisation involved in policy discussions, consultations, or advocacy work? And how?
questions.hint: Informs analysis of: political work, advocacy

questions.text: What policy changes would help organisations like yours succeed? Are there bottlenecks in policy support?
Do local/public authorities support your organisation? How?
Do you collaborate or have partnerships with mainstream enterprises? How?
Do you collaborate with social economy organisations? How?
Have broader societal, economic, technological, environmental trends affected the organisation?
How has the organisation adapted in response?

instruments.title: Assessment Tools (for Managers)
instruments.description: Supplementary questions for WISE managers about current and desired assessment/self-evaluation tools. Part of the WISESHIFT tool co-design process.

questions.text: What kinds of data do you already collect regarding the social and environmental dimensions of your activities?
Do you currently use one or several tools to assess either the social contribution/impact or the ecological contribution/impact of your activities?
If yes: What is this tool (or what are these tools)? What motivates you to use it/them? Are your motivations mainly internal (improvement), external (reporting), or both?
Do you feel a need for such tools? What resources (time, skills, money, training) do you currently allocate to collect such data or use such tools?
What challenges do you experience when collecting such data or using such tools? In your view, what are useful ways to mitigate these challenges?
Are you required to use specific assessment tools or to provide certain social or environmental data (e.g., requested by your funders)?
Do you know of any relevant tools that allow enterprises to self-assess their social and environmental contribution to inclusive and sustainable transition?
Do you feel the need for (additional) assessment tools? What would motivate you to use them? Would your motivations be more internal or external?
Would you be willing to engage with an 'empowering tool' designed not only to assess but also to improve the WISE's contribution to sustainable and inclusive transition?
In your opinion, what key characteristics should this 'empowering tool' have?
What format would be more suitable? (a set of indicators, an online platform, a list of existing tools, a guide, a combination?)
Who should be involved in the use of the tool inside your organisation?
What pros and cons do you see in developing a 'collective dimension' in the use of the tool (i.e. using it not only at the individual organisation level)?
Would you expect the tool to provide a form of internal or external benchmarking?
Do you see more value in standardised tools or in tailored ones? What are the pros and cons of each?
Would you be willing to participate in the co-design of the 'WISESHIFT tool'?
Have you ever heard of [country-specific assessment tools, e.g. SDPI, Social Profit Calculator, etc.]?
Have you ever heard of the impact survey from ENSIE or RREUSE that collects data about the WISEs that they represent?

instruments.title: Board Members / Governing Body
instruments.description: Interview guide for board members or governing body representatives of the WISE.

questions.text: Why did you choose to be part of the board? For how long have you been part of the board? What is your role on the board or governing body of this WISE?
How do you see the mission and strategic direction of the WISE?
questions.hint: Informs: mission framing from governance perspective

questions.text: What are the main strategic challenges currently facing this WISE?
What are the most represented groups in the Board and the GA? How do they contribute to the WISE mission?
Have governance structures, composition, priorities, or decision-making processes changed over time?
How does the board engage with managers, funders, public authorities, or networks?
What (regional, national and/or European) policy or institutional changes would strengthen WISEs like this one?
Could you identify the moments or turning points that you consider the most important in the evolution/development of the WISE?
In what ways would you say that this WISE has a transformative capacity, for its beneficiaries, for the organization itself, and for WISEs or the broader social economy sector?

instruments.title: Founders
instruments.description: Interview guide for the founder(s) of the WISE — capturing the origin story, motivations, and vision.

questions.text: What motivated you to establish this WISE?
questions.hint: Informs: founding motivations, social mission origins

questions.text: How has the WISE changed since it started? What have been the main turning points in the history of the WISE?
What has been the biggest barrier(s) you faced in establishing this WISE?
What achievements of this WISE are you most proud of?
What do you hope the future holds for this WISE?
In what ways would you say that this WISE has a transformative capacity, for its beneficiaries, for the organization itself, and for WISEs or the broader social economy sector?

instruments.title: Funders / Government Officials / Philanthropy
instruments.description: Interview guide for funders, government officials, or philanthropic organisations that support this specific WISE.

questions.text: How would you describe this WISE? Why did your organisation decide to support or fund this WISE? What does your organisation expect in return?
What (regional, national, European) policies or funding frameworks most affect positively or negatively this WISE?
questions.hint: Informs: policy environment from funder view

questions.text: How do you assess this WISE's results or outcomes? If any, list the indicators.
What change, if any, is necessary for the WISEs to continue delivering their mission?
Is your organisation involved in policy discussions, consultations, or advocacy work on behalf of this WISE and SEOs in general?
What public policy changes would help organisations like this WISE succeed? Are there bottlenecks in providing policy support?
In your view, do local/public authorities support this WISE and similar SEOs?
Does this WISE collaborate or have partnerships with mainstream enterprises? How?

instruments.title: WISEs' Networks / Federations
instruments.description: Interview guide for representatives of networks, federations, or NGOs that work with this WISE. Includes questions about assessment tools.

questions.text: What does your organisation do? Please briefly present its mission and main objectives.
How would you describe the WISE – its activities, its mission? Why, how and in what ways does this Network/Federation/NGO collaborate with this WISE?
How does this WISE align with the organisational mission of this Network/Federation/NGO?
Do your members use tools or indicators to assess social or environmental contribution of this WISE?
questions.hint: Informs: assessment practices at network level

questions.text: How could networks/federations/NGOs better support WISEs like this one?
What gaps or needs do you see in your collaboration?
What strengths and challenges do you see in your collaboration?
Are there any policy and regulation that affect positively or negatively the contribution of WISEs?
Does your network/federation/NGO collaborate with other social economy organisations? How? In what ways?
Does this network/federation/NGO collaborate with other stakeholders (civil society, volunteers, customers, NGOs)? How?
In what ways would you say that this WISE has a transformative capacity in relation to other actors?
questions.hint: Informs: transformative power at system level

questions.text: Do you know what kinds of data your members collect regarding the social and environmental dimensions of their activities?
And what about you as a federation/network: do you collect data or use tools linked to social and environmental dimensions of your members' activities?
What challenges do you experience when collecting such data? What are useful ways to mitigate these challenges?
Do you require or recommend that your members use specific tools? Do you request specific social or environmental data from them?
What resources (time, skills, money) are required to collect such data or use such tools? Do you provide training?
In your opinion, what are your members' needs regarding such tools? What would motivate them to use (or not use) such tools?
Do you see more value in standardised tools or in tailored ones? What are the pros and cons of each?
Have you ever heard of [country-specific assessment tools]?
Have you ever heard of the impact survey from ENSIE or RREUSE?

instruments.title: Customers / Service Users
instruments.description: Brief interview guide for customers or service users of the WISE's goods/services.

questions.text: How did you first hear about this WISE?
Why did you decide to use its services or products?
What do you value most about this WISE? How is it different from other service providers in this sector?
Have you noticed any changes in your local community/area since this WISE started? (Optional)
Would you recommend this WISE to others? Why? If not, what could be improved?
Are you familiar with the social, environmental and work integration mission of this WISE? Is the social/environmental mission of this WISE important for you as a customer?

instruments.title: Business Partners
instruments.description: Interview guide for business partners who collaborate with or contract the WISE.

questions.text: How would you describe this WISE (its activities, its mission)? Please describe the nature of your 'partnership' with the WISE.
Why does this business choose to work with this WISE? What were the initial drivers/motivations? For how long have you developed this partnership?
What benefits and challenges does the partnership bring for you and for the WISE?
How has this partnership evolved over time? How do you see the future of this partnership?
What gaps or needs do you see in your partnership? How could you overcome/address them?
Are there (regional, national, European, sectoral) policies or regulations that influence your ability to collaborate with this WISE?
Do you assess the work of this WISE? If yes, do you have indicators? If any, please list them.

instruments.title: Key Informants / Policy Advisers
instruments.description: Interview guide for key informants such as policy advisers, policy development staff, and academics with expertise in WISEs and social economy.

questions.text: What barriers exist to scaling, replicating and/or mainstreaming this type of WISE?
What opportunities currently exist for WISEs? To what extent do these opportunities suggest that WISEs have been influencing mainstream policy or practice?
How have WISEs influenced mainstream policy or practice?
What skills, knowledge, or capacities do you think would help SEOs like this WISE to make better use of the policies or support schemes that already exist?
